mechanism_narrative: A near-zero FEDFUNDS rate keeps policy accommodation in place and supports risk assets by suppressing discount rates and financing costs, so the 10-year Treasury yield rises over the next 20 trading days as equities reprice higher on easier money.
regime_hint: policy rate at the effective lower bound
failure_condition: Reject if, during periods with FEDFUNDS near zero, SPX forward 20-day returns are negative or the realized hit rate for positive returns falls below 50% across the sample.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above a stressed labor-market threshold signals weakening household income and future demand, which compresses equity earnings expectations and pushes the 10-year Treasury yield down over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if, after UNRATE exceeds 7.5%, SPX delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered observations or the average forward return is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and PAYEMS both improve together, retail sales growth and payroll gains confirm broad demand momentum, lifting expected revenues and pushing the 10-year Treasury yield up over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and PAYEMS both rise month-over-month
failure_condition: Reject if joint monthly increases in RSAFS and PAYEMS are not followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the joint signal adds no lift over either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO indicates stronger industrial output and firmer real activity, which increases cyclical growth expectations and pushes the 10-year Treasury yield up over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive month-over-month INDPRO changes fail to predict positive 20-day SPX returns, or if the signal remains weak after controlling for broad market direction.

mechanism_narrative: A low HOUST reading below a housing-activity threshold signals cooling construction demand and weaker future consumer spending, which lowers expected growth and pushes the 10-year Treasury yield down over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if housing starts below 900 do not precede below-median SPX returns over the following 20 trading days, or if the conditional average return is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL is rising while FEDFUNDS remains pinned near zero, real rates fall and liquidity conditions stay easy, which supports equity prices and raises the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises month-over-month while FEDFUNDS stays at or below 0.25%
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-up / policy-flat episodes do not outperform other periods in forward SPX returns, or if the interaction term is insignificant once each series is included separately.

mechanism_narrative: A declining PAYEMS trend over a 3-month window signals labor-market deterioration and weaker household demand, which reduces earnings expectations and pushes the 10-year Treasury yield down over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if three-month payroll declines are not followed by negative 20-day SPX returns more often than chance, or if the average forward return is not below zero.

mechanism_narrative: In a weak-growth regime with UNRATE elevated and GDPC1 still below trend, any improvement in RSAFS is interpreted as temporary inventory or price effects rather than durable demand, so the signal should fail to lift the 10-year Treasury yield and equities should still fall over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE > 7.0% and GDPC1 is below its prior-year level
failure_condition: Reject if, under elevated unemployment and below-year-ago GDP, positive RSAFS surprises are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time or the interaction effect is not negative.
